skill.name: darkit-gin
skill.description: Darkit Gin guidance for building Go APIs with gin-gonic/gin, including JWT authentication, SSE realtime events, cache usage, middleware, OpenAPI documentation, and production patterns.
skill.summary: Build and document production-ready Go APIs with Darkit Gin patterns.
skill.author: DarkiT

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced a high volume of findings, but review shows the command execution, weak cryptography, network, and filesystem hits are mainly Markdown examples and API names rather than executable skill behavior. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration intent was found. The skill is publishable with warnings because several examples could encourage unsafe production choices if copied without hardening.

medium_findings.title: Copyable Production Insecurity in Examples
medium_findings.description: The examples include wildcard CORS and hardcoded Docker Compose database credentials. They are documentation examples, but users could copy them into production without replacing insecure defaults.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risky patterns are directly present in documentation examples. The same lines include context that one setting should be restricted in production, so this is guidance risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Browser Token Storage Example Requires Warning
medium_findings.description: The JWT guide shows localStorage token storage in a client example. The FAQ recommends HttpOnly cookies, but the copyable client snippet still needs a clear XSS warning.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The localStorage write is explicit and security-sensitive. The surrounding FAQ lowers severity because it also identifies cookies as safer and notes localStorage requires XSS defenses.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The numerous shell command findings are fenced documentation examples for curl, OpenAPI Generator, and local testing. They are not executable skill hooks or scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are inside Markdown code fences and describe local developer commands. I found no evidence that the skill executes these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are API Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits point to words such as Description, PathParam, QueryParam, Header, gzip, and JWT documentation rather than actual weak hash or cipher implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of representative high-severity lines shows API documentation and compression examples. No evidence found for MD5, SHA1, DES, or custom cryptographic implementation at those cited lines.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Path Traversal Finding Is a Relative Documentation Link
low_findings.description: The path traversal alert points to a Markdown link that references a parent documentation page. It is not used for file reads or writes by executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a documentation link, not a runtime filesystem operation. No evidence found that the skill follows or reads this path programmatically.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Search Found No Evidence
low_findings.description: Review found no instruction text that asks the evaluator or user to ignore prior instructions, override system rules, skip review, or treat the skill as pre-approved.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search for common injection phrases returned no suspicious matches. The cited section contains normal usage guidance for the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Fenced Shell and Network Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains copyable curl and generator commands that contact localhost URLs. These are expected for API documentation but should remain user-reviewed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are real shell examples but are not executed by the skill. Risk depends on user copy-paste behavior and local endpoint trust.

dangerous_patterns.title: Public Endpoint Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents OpenAPI, Swagger, metrics, health, and SSE endpoints. These features are legitimate but require access control decisions in production systems.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint examples are clear and legitimate framework behavior. Exposure risk depends on deployment configuration rather than malicious skill intent.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent guidance when building Go APIs with Gin. This skill provides Darkit Gin patterns for routing, authentication, realtime events, caching, middleware, and API documentation.
actual_capabilities: Explains Darkit Gin router setup with options for JWT, cache, CORS, rate limits, and request IDs.
Provides route, CRUD, response helper, and API versioning patterns for Go services.
Guides JWT session creation, middleware checks, token refresh flows, and client usage.
Documents SSE hubs, realtime event publishing, client listeners, and monitoring examples.
Covers cache configuration, compressed cache values, sharding, invalidation, and cleanup patterns.
Shows OpenAPI metadata, route annotations, Swagger UI setup, and client generation workflows.
limitations: The skill is documentation-focused and does not install or execute Darkit Gin code by itself.
Examples need security review before production use, especially CORS, token storage, and credentials.
Some referenced resources are listed but not present in this skill package.
Guidance assumes Go and gin-gonic/gin familiarity.

use_cases.title: Create a New Go API
use_cases.description: Use the quick start and API patterns to scaffold routes, responses, middleware, and service structure.

use_cases.title: Add Authentication and Authorization
use_cases.description: Apply JWT session helpers, protected route groups, refresh tokens, and role checks to an existing Gin service.

use_cases.title: Publish API Documentation
use_cases.description: Use OpenAPI annotations and Swagger UI guidance to produce readable API documentation for consumers.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Darkit Gin Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a basic Darkit Gin API with health checks, request IDs, rate limits, and one ping endpoint.

prompt_templates.title: Add JWT Protection
prompt_templates.prompt: Add JWT login, protected route middleware, refresh token handling, and secure production notes to my Darkit Gin API.

prompt_templates.title: Design Realtime Events
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an SSE notification feature for Darkit Gin with event channels, reconnect handling, and safe client parsing.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Darkit Gin service for production readiness across CORS, secrets, JWT storage, caching, OpenAPI exposure, and observability.

output_examples.input: I need JWT authentication for a Darkit Gin API.
output_examples.output: A structured implementation plan covering login, token creation, protected middleware, refresh flow, secure cookie storage, and required environment secrets.

output_examples.input: Document my user routes with OpenAPI.
output_examples.output: A route documentation checklist with summaries, parameters, response descriptions, security scheme references, and Swagger UI setup steps.

output_examples.input: Add realtime notifications to my API.
output_examples.output: An SSE design outline with server event channels, message types, client listeners, reconnect behavior, and monitoring considerations.

best_practices: Replace example secrets, local URLs, and default database credentials before deployment.
Use HttpOnly secure cookies for browser JWT storage when possible.
Restrict CORS origins, OpenAPI access, metrics endpoints, and SSE channels in production.
anti_patterns: Do not copy wildcard CORS settings into production services.
Do not store long-lived JWTs in localStorage without a clear XSS mitigation plan.
Do not expose generated OpenAPI, metrics, or realtime endpoints without access controls.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build Go web APIs using Darkit Gin patterns for routing, middleware, authentication, caching, SSE, and OpenAPI documentation.

faq.question: Does the skill run shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The package contains Markdown guidance and code examples. Static command findings come from fenced examples, not executable scripts.

faq.question: Is it safe to use the examples directly?
faq.answer: Use them as starting points. Review secrets, CORS, token storage, database credentials, and public endpoints before production use.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it cover API documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes OpenAPI configuration, route annotations, Swagger UI setup, and client generation guidance.

faq.question: Does it include deployment guidance?
faq.answer: It includes configuration and Docker Compose examples, but teams must harden those examples for their own environment.
